mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative repricing in S&P 500 equities over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue expansion and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive directional accuracy above 50% over 60-day horizons when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side efficiency, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a directional accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect sustained economic activity and wage growth, which directly boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase above 0.4% signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in tighter future monetary policy and higher discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of cases when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales validates robust aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than half of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates supply chain bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing demand, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases.
